note: Cold spec-judge round 1 returned ALIGNED false. I had leaked the literal word 'seat' twice into the metaphor-free package (mail/doorbell/README.md and unit/registry/README.md) while writing the very non-goals meant to hold the boundary. Same class as the #159 Bunker leak the owner forced a full revert of. Both parentheticals deleted outright — the preceding clause ('the unit that stands in for it') already carried the meaning, so they added zero information. Round 2 fresh-context judge: metaphor grep clean on every added line.

Cold spec-judge round 1 also found two suite-form defects. (1) A doorbell Then step asserted an internal call ('focus is never probed') rather than an observable outcome — deleted; the preceding pane-rung assertion already proves the behavior against a non-focused fixture. (2) The scenario pinning the spawn-capability gate was unfalsifiable: its Given never varied subagent-ness, so an implementation that DID branch on it would still pass. Rewritten as a 2x2 Scenario Outline (realization x probe) so the outcome demonstrably tracks the probe and is invariant across realization. Round 2 judged the rewrite genuinely falsifiable. Also fixed a pre-existing 'ship' metaphor leak that a reflow had turned into a +line in this diff.

Cold impl-judge returned IMPLEMENTATION_PASS false on a real structural defect it reproduced live: wakeRecipient resolved the presence by handle through resolveStandingOwner (throw-capable) OUTSIDE its swallowing try, so a standing record removed concurrently mid-call let an uncaught throw escape and crash `mail send` AFTER durable delivery — violating the frozen cross-cutting contract 'this never throws — it can never fail the send'. All 15 per-scenario checks were green; the blocker was structural, caught only by the judge's orthogonal read. Fixed by splitting a record-based, throw-incapable presenceOf() core out of resolvePresence and having the doorbell use it, plus a regression test proving the mid-call disappearance no longer throws.

Cold impl-judge mutation-tested the 2x2 Scenario Outline's TEST and proved it a weak discriminator (the #211 class): the realization column only reached the caller's own handle, never claimPresence, so injecting an isSubagent-style env branch into claimPresence left 70/70 tests passing — zero regression protection against exactly the anti-pattern the scenario's title forbids. Fixed in the TEST, not the frozen .feature: the two realization rows now carry genuinely different signals (spawnedBy set + subagent-ish harness env markers vs neither), varied independently of the probe, so the same mutation now fails the suite and the invariance claim is earned rather than tautological.
